title: AST Assault 2 DV: North Pole [AK25117304]
summary: On November 22, 2025, at approximately 13:21 hours, AST responded to a report of assault (Domestic Violence) in North Pole. The suspect in this case was apprehended and remanded to FCC on charges of assault in the 2nd degree. The investigation is ongoing.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: AST Assault 3/MIW 5: North Pole [AK25117251]
summary: On 11/22/2025, AST and MCT responded to an address in North Pole for a report of an individual having a mental health event. Upon arrival, Chadwick Vance (35 yoa) of North Pole pointed a firearm at MCT personnel. Chadwick was arrested and remanded to FCC for Assault 3 and MIW 5.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: AST Homicide Investigation: Fairbanks [AK25117178]
summary: Update: On December 22, 2025, Jacob Marcy, age 33, of Fairbanks, was arrested in connection with Boyd’s death on November 22, 2025. The Alaska Bureau of Investigation identified him as a suspect during their investigation into the shooting death. Marcy was arrested without incident and charged with murder in the first degree. We would like to thank the public for their assistance in this case. Original: On November 22, 2025, at 12:55 am, the Alaska State Troopers were notified that a deceased male was located in a vehicle near a gas station on Farmer's Loop in the Fairbanks area. Troopers responded and located 37-year-old Michael Boyd in the driver's seat of a vehicle deceased. Troopers believe that this is a homicide, and the Alaska Bureau of Investigation has assumed case responsibility.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

entities.address_detail: identified him as a suspect during their investigation into the shooting death

title: City council supports proposed timber sales, narrows city manager search
summary: Ketchikan’s city hall on June 11, 2020 (KRBD file photo by Maria Dudzak) The Ketchikan City Council voted to support two proposed timber harvests on borough-owned land at the council’s Thursday night meeting. The council also discussed applicants for a vacant city and utility manager position, and approved two budget transfers and a collective bargaining agreement. The proposed timber sales from Ketchikan-based A...

title: State Nets $17.5M in North Slope Lease Sale
summary: HOME | Page 13 State Nets $17.5M in North Slope Lease Sale Tracts all across the North Slope lease area drew the interest of nine bidding companies. Not shown on this map, one bidder each in the Beaufort Sea lease area and the Foothills to the south also secured leases. Photo Credit: Alaska Department of Natural Resources Companies new to the Alaska oil patch participated in the state’s annual North Slope lease sale, resulting in the most tracts leased since 2014. The Alaska Division of Oil & Gas calls the outcome the most successful in more than a decade in terms of acrea...

title: Waning immunity and falling vaccination rates fuel pertussis outbreaks
summary: A sign, seen July 11, 2025, directs visitors to the entrance of Alaska Regional Hospital entrance and adjacent medical buildings. (Photo by Yereth Rosen/Alaska Beacon) Rates of pertussis, also known as whooping cough, are surging in Texas, Florida, California, Oregon, and other states and localities across the country. The outbreaks are fueled by falling vaccination rates, fading immunity, and delays in public health tracking systems, according to interviews with state and federal health officials. Babies too young to be fully vaccinated are most at risk. “Pertussis cases increase in a cyclical fashion driven by waning immunity, but the size of the outbreak and the potential for severe outcomes in children who cannot be vaccinated can be mitigated by high coverage and good communi...

title: CILC Receives $200K Grant from KeyBank as Bicentennial Birthday Gift
summary: HOME | Page 14 CILC Receives $200K Grant from KeyBank as Bicentennial Birthday Gift KeyBank Alaska Market President Lori McCaffrey (center) presents a $200,000 grant to Cook Inlet Lending Center CEO and President Jeff Tickle (pictured to her left). Also pictured are Financial Wellness Program Manager Abby Miller, KeyBank Business Banking Relationship Manager Garet Plantz and Regional Corporate Responsibility Officer I.V. Reeves. Photo Credit: KeyBank About 200 years after the oldest city in New York was est...

title: AST Disturbance: Moose Pass [AK25116752]
summary: On 11/20/2025, Seward AST received a call of a disturbance in Moose Pass at approximately 1900 hours. AST responded and found the complainant(s) in an active struggle with the suspect, Liam Mcfaul, in the bedroom of the residence. As Mcfaul was placed under arrest for Criminal Mischief, Mcfaul began to injure himself in the back a patrol unit causing a laceration to his forehead. Mcfaul was remanded to Spring Creek Correction Center for Assault 4 - Injury, two counts of Resisting Arrest, Criminal Mischief, and Disorderly Conduct.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch
